renderedFields.description:
Description
We need to add support for opening the cashier at a predefined step and prefill the amount field.
To enable this, the frontend must read two new parameters:

  • cashierstep — defines which step the cashier should open on
    • depositcrypto
    • buycrypto
    • withdrawcrypto
  • cashiersum — prefilled amount (number)
    • Applies only to flows that have an amount field (e.g., buycrypto)
    • Does not apply to depositcrypto (no amount field)

Both parameters (cashier*step* and cashier*sum*) must be passed to the backend together with the existing cashier payload.

Optional requirement
When the cashier is closed, the frontend should clear the values of the two new parameters (cashier*step* and cashier*sum*) so they do not persist between sessions.

Expected result

  • Frontend reads the two new parameters from the URL
  • These parameters are included in all relevant backend requests
  • Cashier opens on the correct step
  • Amount field is prefilled when applicable
  • (Optional) Parameters are cleared on cashier close

comments.body: The task was moved to hold due to the dev and QA environments not working.

The task has been put on hold due to the absence of the required implementation on the PaymentHub side, which prevents proper testing from our end. The task will be resumed once the corresponding PaymentHub functionality is implemented.

Galadriel FYI
link to discussion
https://git.dolore.cc/portal/portal/-/merge_requests/154

Tested on the qa env

Devices: iPhone 15 Pro Max, MAcBook AIr

Browsers: Safari, Chrome

Validet scenarios:

  • cashierstep=depositcrypto
  • cashierstep=buycrypto
  • cashierstep=buycrypto&cashiersum=100
  • cashierstep=withdrawcrypto
  • cashierstep=invalid

Was checked for unauthorized and authorized users

fields.summary: [FE] read and pass cashierstep, cashiersum parameters

renderedFields.description:
Environment:

  • Prod: https://minebit-casino.prod.cp.nextcode.tech/my-account/profile/info
  • Pixel 6, iPhone 15 Pro Max, MacBook Air
  • Chrome

STR:

  1. Navigate to the user profile page.
  2. Ensure an email address is displayed in the email input field.
  3. Click the “Verify Email” button.

AR:

The email address disappears from the input field after clicking “Verify Email.”

https://www.awesomescreenshot.com/video/43851965?key=f9dd47dceff8f27b5aaf5ad1dc8e331a

ER:

The email address remains in the input field after clicking “Verify Email.”

comments.body:
Tested on the qa env

Devices: MacBook Air, iPhone 15 Pro Max

Browsers: Safari, Chrome

Work as expected

fields.summary: Email input field is cleared when clicking “Verify Email” button on user profile page

content.text: Pixel 6, iPhone 15 Pro Max, MacBook Air
Navigate to the user profile page.
Ensure an email address is displayed in the email input field.
The email address disappears from the input field after clicking “Verify Email.”
The email address remains in the input field after clicking “Verify Email.”
Devices: MacBook Air, iPhone 15 Pro Max
